articles.headline: ZTE Highlights Path to Broadband Monetization at FTTH Conference 2026
articles.summary: ZTE outlined strategies for broadband providers to monetize networks amid rising data demands from AI and cloud services. This offers SMEs and enterprises tools to enhance connectivity revenue streams and reduce infrastructure costs. Operational efficiency gains are possible for businesses upgrading digital tools.
articles.imageUrl: https://www.zte.com.cn/content/dam/zte-site/res-www-zte-com-cn/国际新闻/微信分享缩略图4/ZTE Highlights Path to Broadband Monetization at FTTH Conference 2026.jpg

articles.headline: Rise in Ransomware Attacks Hits Energy and Manufacturing Sectors
articles.summary: Gunra Ransomware targeted Thai Petroleum & Trading, while The Gentlemen Ransomware struck Aichi Electric Co., causing data loss, financial damage, and disruptions in energy and manufacturing. These incidents highlight escalating cybersecurity risks for critical infrastructure and supply chains. Businesses must prioritize defenses to avoid operational halts and reputational harm.

articles.headline: U.S. Utilities Gear Up for $1.4 Trillion Spending Surge to Power AI Data Centers
articles.summary: America’s largest utilities plan $1.4 trillion in spending over five years to meet AI-driven electricity demand and upgrade grids, impacting cloud computing costs and infrastructure economics. This will reshape supply chains for chips and large-model AI deployment while creating new regional tech corridors. Businesses face higher energy costs but opportunities in expanded compute-adjacent real estate and industrial policy.
